era: 2014-present. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Indie Electronic. Kim West (vocals, synthesizers) and Ryan Devlin (guitar, production). Self-released. Moody synth-pop with atmospheric production. E=5. Genre Stage 97 (Indie Electronic enters). Capitol Hill returns.

location.origin: Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington. The historic center of Seattle's counterculture, queer identity, live music, and DIY art-making.
location.formative: Capitol Hill's small venue circuit: Barboza, Neumos, Sunset Tavern, house shows. KEXP sessions for broader exposure.
location.career: Smokey Brights -- Kim West and Ryan Devlin. Capitol Hill duo. Synth-forward, atmospheric, self-released. Three albums: Wild Rites (2016), I Love You But Damn (2018), Time Is a Crooked Bow (2020).

associated_musicians.name: Mark Lanegan
associated_musicians.note: Darkwave to indie electronic. Both dark, atmospheric. Lanegan vocal; Smokey Brights environmental.

associated_musicians.name: Loomer
associated_musicians.note: Both atmospheric density. Loomer through guitar pedals; Smokey Brights through synthesizers.

associated_musicians.name: Kinski
associated_musicians.note: Both Capitol Hill, both E=5, both rock/electronic boundary. Kinski motorik; Smokey Brights synth-pop.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Scholars
associated_musicians.note: Both self-released Capitol Hill. Different genres, same ethic.

theory_nodes.concept: The moody pulse (one hundred twenty-second acoustic element -- throbbing moderate-tempo electronic pulse embedded in dense atmospheric production; dark and propulsive; introspective and physical; synthesizer density with human vocal anchor; the rhythmic foundation of indie electronic)
examples: Smokey Brights (degree 121) -- indie electronic. E=5. The moody pulse. Synth-forward atmospheric production.
Moderate-tempo electronic pulse: drum machines at moderate BPM. Not dance tempo, not ambient tempo.
Synthesizer density: thick layers of analog-inflected textures. Dense but not cluttered.
Human vocal anchor: Kim West's smoky delivery as the organic center of electronic architecture.
theory_nodes.description: The moody pulse is the 122nd acoustic element. Throbbing electronic atmosphere with dark momentum.

ancestors: The bark and the whisper (degree 120) -- vocal-centric. The moody pulse is production-centric.
The near-silence (degree 119) -- E=2 ambient. The moody pulse (E=5) returns density and forward motion.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred twenty-two acoustic elements across one hundred twenty-one degrees. Track element 123 at degree 122.

theory_nodes.concept: Capitol Hill Returns (geographic pattern -- Capitol Hill streak 110-115, departure 116-120, return 121; the neighborhood as gravitational center; the engine orbits Capitol Hill)
examples: Departure: 116 Seattle proper, 117-119 Seattle generic, 120 Ellensburg.
Capitol Hill as gravitational center -- the engine orbits it.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill Returns at degree 121. The neighborhood is the engine's gravitational center.

ancestors: Capitol Hill streak (degrees 110-115). The departure at 116. Return at 121.
genealogy.relationship: Geographic pattern. Capitol Hill orbit confirmed. Track future Capitol Hill entries.

theory_nodes.concept: Indie Electronic enters (Genre Stage 97 -- synth-forward production with indie songwriting structures; synthesizers serve songs, not dancefloors; moody, atmospheric, song-based; Layer 3 of Capitol Hill's electronic scene)
examples: Smokey Brights (degree 121): indie electronic, synth-pop-influenced, self-released.
Layer 3 of Capitol Hill electronic: not dance (Layer 1), not experimental (Layer 2), but indie-electronic synthesis.
Synthesizers create atmospheric beds; vocals inhabit them; guitar adds organic texture.
97th genre in the engine. Indie Electronic is distinct from prior electronic genres.
theory_nodes.description: Indie Electronic enters at Genre Stage 97. Synth-forward songwriting, atmospheric and song-based.

ancestors: Darkwave/Indie (degree 120, Genre Stage 96). Electronic elements in prior degrees.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 97 at degree 121. Track recurrence of indie electronic.

cross_references.relationship: DARKNESS CONTINUITY. Darkwave (120, E=4) to indie electronic (121, E=5). Both dark, atmospheric, reverb-laden. Lanegan's darkness vocal; Smokey Brights' environmental.
SHOEGAZE/ELECTRONIC ADJACENCY. Both atmospheric density creators. Loomer through guitar pedals; Smokey Brights through synthesizers.
CAPITOL HILL ELECTRONIC. Both Capitol Hill, both E=5, both rock/electronic boundary. Kinski motorik drone; Smokey Brights synth-pop atmosphere.
CAPITOL HILL DIY. Both self-released Capitol Hill artists. Different genres (hip-hop vs indie electronic), same ethic, same neighborhood.
POST-CAPITOL-HILL RETURN. Last non-Capitol-Hill Seattle entry before geographic wandering of 117-120. Smokey Brights at 121 brings the engine back.
